text:

As noted previously, each data provider applies its own classification methodology and internal criteria, meaning these changes require ongoing education and can take time to be implemented.

This marks another positive step forward in ensuring that all market participants and potential investors have a clear understanding of who we are and what we do.

text:
The Smarter Web Company
Weekly Investor Snapshot: Week Ending 19 June 2026

Share Price Performance
SWC outperformed the broader Bitcoin treasury sector this week, closing +3.7%. Peer performance was mixed, with Metaplanet +2%, Strive +1%, Strategy -6%, and CapitalB -13.5%. 

It was constructive to see SWC trade back above 30p mid-week for the first time since the end of May. Note we had three announcements during the week:

• A subscription update raising approximately £0.3m.
• The promotion of Oliver Hewett from Group Financial Controller to CFO.
• Shareholder approval of our proposed capital reduction.

Our Fully Diluted EV/BTC Value ratio closed the week at 0.87x (same as the previous Friday).

Volume
Trading activity was quieter this week, with approximately 5.5m shares traded across our three listings, equivalent to 1.7% of our Effective Shares Outstanding* (versus 9.4m shares traded the previous week). Note also the US market was closed on 19 June.

This was the lowest weekly trading volume since the w/c 16 February, when 4.8m shares were traded. However, combined with SWC's positive share price performance, this may indicate that recent selling pressure is beginning to moderate.

Market Colour
Bitcoin closed the week down 4%* and remains within its recent trading range of approximately $60,000–$65,000.

Encouragingly, Bitcoin continues to hold above the $60,000 level lending support to recent market commentary that this area may represent a local bottom. In the near term, a reclaim of the 50-day moving average (approximately $65,750) would be a small positive for market sentiment.

The broader market tone remained cautious throughout the week, driven by a combination of hawkish Fed commentary, ongoing uncertainty surrounding US-Iran negotiations, and continued focus on the price action of STRC US (Strategy's perpetual preferred equity).

There was significant discussion across the market regarding STRC and readers seeking additional context should review commentary from Jesse Myers, our Head of Bitcoin Strategy (@Croesus_BTC).

Looking ahead, market attention is likely to remain focused on the performance of STRC (and SATA), alongside Thursday's US PCE inflation print. A higher-than-expected reading could reinforce the market's current "higher for longer" narrative for rates whereas a lower print would be positive for markets.

LSE: SWC | OTCQB: TSWCF | FRA: 3M8

Disclaimer: Personal market observations only.

* Bitcoin performance references are measured from the opening of UK equity markets on Monday to the close of UK equity markets on Friday.

* Effective Shares Outstanding: 323,813,315 (Shares in issue - ATM unsold), (371,965,705- 48,152,390)
* Fully Diluted Shares (used for Bitcoin treasury analytics): 366,835,598

text:
Shareholders today overwhelmingly approved the proposed capital reduction, with 99.94% of votes cast in favour. 

Subject to Court approval, currently expected on 14 July, the capital reduction is expected to become effective on or around 15 July 2026.

This is expected to create distributable reserves, providing the Company with greater flexibility to pursue future corporate actions, including initiatives that may support shareholder value and our long-term strategy.

Thank you to all Smarter Web shareholders who voted and to everyone for your continued support.

text:
The Smarter Web Company
Weekly Investor Snapshot: Week Ending 12 June 2026

Share Price Performance
SWC closed the week -2.6%. Performance across the Bitcoin treasury sector was mixed, with Strive +4.5%, Strategy broadly flat, CapitalB -3%, and Metaplanet -5%.

This week we updated metrics on our analytics page, with our Fully Diluted EV / BTC Value ratio closing the week at 0.87x. For further commentary, please refer to our CEO Andrew Webley's weekly update (@asjwebley).

Volume
Trading activity remained healthy, with 9.05m shares traded across our three listings, equivalent to 2.8% of our Effective Shares oustanding*, compared to 9.4m shares traded the previous week.

Notably, this was the highest volume week for our US listing (1.28m shares traded) since the w/c 20 April (1.6m shares). Despite continued volatility, trading volumes remained robust, reflecting sustained investor engagement and liquidity.

Market Colour
Bitcoin closed the week broadly unchanged*, ending close to where it began.

Encouragingly, the price remained above the $60,000 level throughout the week, supporting feedback we received previously that technical and positioning data suggested a local bottom may be forming.

Market sentiment was also helped by reports that a potential Iran-related agreement could be approaching, which contributed to a weaker US dollar and supported risk assets.

SWC traded as low as 26p during the week, at one stage down approximately 11% versus the prior week's close, before recovering strongly and finishing well off the lows. Friday's 6.5% gain was an encouraging sign that recent selling pressure may be beginning to ease.

Throughout the week there was a significant amount of market attention and capital focused on SpaceX and other momentum opportunities. As a result, Bitcoin and related equities may have been used as a source of liquidity for investors seeking gains elsewhere. 

My personal view remains that a portion of money made in SpaceX and US equities will end up back rotating into Bitcoin. Next week attention will remain focused on price action of SATA and STRC and when they return to their $100 par value.

LSE: SWC | OTCQB: TSWCF | FRA: 3M8

Disclaimer: Personal market observations only.

*Bitcoin performance references are measured from the opening of UK equity markets on Monday to the close of UK equity markets on Friday.
* Effective Shares Outstanding: 322,746,315 (Shares in issue - ATM unsold), (371,965,705 - 49,219,390)
* Fully Diluted Shares (used for Bitcoin treasury analytics): 365,768,598

As Smarter Web continues to grow, it's important that investors and market participants understand who we are and what we do.

Many data providers operate within their own classification frameworks and editorial guidelines, so ensuring our business is accurately represented requires ongoing engagement and education.

Following previous work around our FTSE index classifications, Bloomberg has now updated our company description to better reflect our current operations and Bitcoin treasury strategy.

We're continuing to engage with platforms and data providers to improve awareness and understanding of Smarter Web, and we'll keep shareholders updated as further progress is made.

text:
The Smarter Web Company
Weekly Investor Snapshot: Week Ending 05 June 2026

Share Price Performance
SWC closed the week down -5.8%. Despite broader sector weakness, SWC outperformed peers for the second consecutive week. CapitalB was -12.6% whilst Strive, Strategy and Metaplanet were all down approximately 19-20% over the same period*. Encouragingly, our mNAV periodically traded above 1.0x during the week and closed at 1.0x.

Volume
Trading activity remained healthy, with 9.4m shares traded across our three listings, equivalent to 3.1% of our effective share count*, compared with 8.7m shares the previous week. This was the highest weekly volume since w/c 04 May (9.9m shares traded), suggesting that despite weaker price action, market engagement remains strong.

A small note: the FTSE had the snapshot of it's quarterly index review on Tuesday. SWC remains a constituent of both the FTSE Small Cap and FTSE All-Share indices. Net buying flows expected on 19 June are minimal, with both the review outcome and associated flows inline with expectations.

Feedback / Market Colour
This was Bitcoin's worst weekly performance since FTX, driven by a combination of factors including ongoing negative momentum, key technical support levels breaking, weakness in the Nasdaq following its recent rally and Broadcom's softer AI outlook, and what I believe was misinformed market interpretations of Strategy's sale of 32 Bitcoin as part of treasury management.

The key question remains whether Bitcoin has reached a bottom. Bitcoin is now trading just above its 200-week moving average (~$61,800). Historically, major cycle lows have tended to occur around this level, although market conditions differ across cycles.

Separately, I spoke with a large crypto-native market maker whose internal models suggest Bitcoin is beginning to form a base from a positioning and technical perspective. However, they noted that the scope of potential upside is likely to depend on developments in geopolitics and subsequent interest rate expectations.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

Disclaimer: Personal market observations only.  

*Effective share count = Issued shs (353,203,102) - ATM unsold (49,721,640) = 303,481,462.

*Data sourced from TradingView

text:
Following up on yesterday’s RNS titled “Proposed Capital Reduction and Notice of General Meeting”.

There’s been a lot of interesting analysis, commentary and discussion on X from the community and other stakeholders post the release.

Naturally, there are limits to what we can say beyond the information already published, but we believe this represents an important step for the Company.

I recognise that it has been a challenging period for the share price, particularly against a backdrop of weaker Bitcoin sentiment. However, if approved, this proposal would provide the Company with greater flexibility and a broader range of options to pursue potential, future opportunities as they arise.

We would therefore encourage all shareholders to vote and, as always, thank you for your continued support.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

engagement.note: These figures were correct as of June 3, 2026, 1:22 AM London time

text:
Thought this was an interesting take.

There's no getting around it: short-term sentiment for Bitcoin is challenged. Sticky inflation, interest rate expectations, technical levels, quantum fud and capital chasing other opportunities have all weighed on price action. The Fear & Greed Index currently sits at 29 ("Fear").

I've been here before. I remember sitting on the Galaxy trading floor throughout 2022 wondering whether we'd ever see new all-time highs again after a variety of events that hit confidence. Eventually, we did.

My view remains unchanged: if you believe fiat debasement will continue over the long term, there remains a strong structural case for Bitcoin.

The challenge is that markets often gravitate towards whatever is working at the time - whether that's AI, US tech, Korean equities or another strong-performing theme. Capital follows momentum, and when that happens Bitcoin can temporarily fall out of favour, even if the long-term thesis remains intact.

Despite that, corporates continue to accumulate. Smarter Web is one of them.

Our recent conference in Bristol reinforced just how much activity is taking place across the Bitcoin treasury sector. For now, patience and perseverance is required.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

text:
RNS out regarding how many pre-IPO warrants have been exercised since the warrant window opened on 24 April 2026.

18,762,603 warrants have been exercised at 2.5p, generating approximately £469k of cash for the Company and resulting in the issuance of 18,762,603 new shares.

As a result, the total number of shares in issue has increased from 353,203,102 to 371,965,705.

35,303,732 warrants now remain outstanding. Approximately 27.2m are held by Andrew Webley, his spouse, directors and employees, leaving only approximately 8.1m warrants held by other parties.

As a reminder, the Company originally had 96,066,335 warrants outstanding. Since then, 42m warrants were removed through discounted warrant buybacks, while a further 18.8m have been exercised (as per today's announcement).

The exercise period remains open until 24 April 2028 and The Company will provide another update in due course.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8
